content.text: Answer seven problems. Write your answers clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote results (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

body.text: Prove that every field has an algebraic closure.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Determine a splitting field \(K\) for the polynomial \(X^8-1\in\mathbb{Q}[X]\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Determine \(\operatorname{Gal}(K/\mathbb{Q})\) and give an explicit description of the action of \(\operatorname{Gal}(K/\mathbb{Q})\) on \(K\).

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: For each subgroup \(H\) of \(\operatorname{Gal}(K/\mathbb{Q})\) find the subfield of \(K\) fixed by \(H\).

body.text: Let \(E\) and \(F\) be fields such that \(\operatorname{char}(E)\ne\operatorname{char}(F)\). Let \(V\) be a vector space over \(E\) and let \(W\) be a vector space over \(F\). Prove that \(V\otimes_{\mathbb{Z}}W\) is trivial.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Give the definition of a covariant functor from a category \(C\) to a category \(D\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Give the definition of a concrete category.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Define what it means for an object \(F\) in a concrete category to be free on a set \(S\).

subparts.label: (d)
subparts.text: Given a set \(S\), construct a free object on \(S\) in the category of pointed sets.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define what it means for an \(R\)-module \(M\) to be injective.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Let \(\{M_\lambda:\lambda\in\Lambda\}\) be a nonempty collection of injective \(R\)-modules. Prove that the product \(\prod_{\lambda\in\Lambda}M_\lambda\) is injective.

body.text: Let \(F\) be a free group on a set \(S\) with \(|S|>1\). Prove that \(F\) is not solvable.
Let \(R\) be a commutative Noetherian ring with \(1\) and let \(I\subset R\) be an ideal. Prove that \(I\) has a primary decomposition.

Let \(S\) be a commutative ring with \(1\), let \(R\) be a subring of \(S\) which contains \(1_S\), and let \(x\in S\). Suppose there is a subring \(T\subset S\) such that \(T\supset R[x]\) and \(T\) is finitely generated as an \(R\)-module. Prove that \(x\) is integral over \(R\).

Prove Noether’s Normalization Lemma: Let \(k\) be a field and let \(A\) be a finitely generated \(k\)-algebra with \(1\). Then there are \(y_1,\ldots,y_q\in A\) such that \(y_1,\ldots,y_q\) are algebraically independent over \(k\) and \(A\) is integral over \(k[y_1,\ldots,y_q]\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that every nonzero proper ideal in a Dedekind domain is a product of prime ideals.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Give an example of an integral domain \(R\) and a nonzero proper ideal \(I\subset R\) such that \(I\) cannot be expressed as a product of prime ideals.

body.text: Let \(G\) be a finite group of order \(n\) and let \(F\) be a field.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(\operatorname{char}(F)\nmid n\) then for every \(F[G]\)-module \(V\) and every \(F[G]\)-submodule \(W\subset V\) there is an \(F[G]\)-submodule \(W'\subset V\) such that \(V=W\oplus W'\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Show that if \(\operatorname{char}(F)\mid n\) then there exists an \(F[G]\)-module \(V\) and an \(F[G]\)-submodule \(W\subset V\) such that there is no \(F[G]\)-submodule \(W'\subset V\) such that \(V=W\oplus W'\).
